title: A2A Protocol
description: A2AAgent wraps a remote agent behind AIAgent; AddA2AServer + MapA2AHttpJson expose agents via the A2A protocol.

steps.annotation: Server side: register the agent, attach an A2A server, and map HTTP+JSON endpoints.
The server publishes an AgentCard at GET /card — callers discover capabilities.

steps.content: IA2AClient a2aClient = new A2AClient(new Uri("http://localhost:5161/a2a/weather"), http);
steps.annotation: Client side: create an A2AClient pointing at the server's A2A endpoint.
AsAIAgent() wraps the remote agent as a local AIAgent — full MAF integration.

steps.content: What is the weather in London?
steps.annotation: The user sends a message to the local A2AAgent.
A2AAgent.RunAsync translates the call into an A2A JSON-RPC request to the server.

steps.content: Server → ChatClientAgent.RunAsync("What is the weather in London?")
steps.annotation: The server's A2AAgentHandler delegates to the hosted ChatClientAgent.

steps.content: The weather in London is 15°C and cloudy.
steps.annotation: The response flows back through the A2A protocol to the A2AAgent client.

steps.content:
remoteAgent.AsAIFunction() → can be used as a tool in another agent
AgentWorkflowBuilder.BuildSequential([localAgent, remoteAgent])
steps.annotation: A2AAgent IS-A AIAgent — compose it as a tool, in a workflow, or any MAF orchestration.
